name: Godot Engine
description: Free, open-source game engine with its own GDScript language plus C# support. Covers 2D and 3D workflows with a scene tree architecture, animation editor, and shaders—no royalties.

seo.title: Godot Engine — Game Development Tools
seo.meta_description: Godot Engine: Free, open-source game engine with its own GDScript language plus C# support. Covers 2D and 3D workflows with a scene tree architecture, animation

when_to_use: Open-source game prototype contributed to community showcase
GDScript rapid prototype validated before engine-switch decision
C# game module integrated into larger Godot project
Browser-exported HTML5 game embedded on personal portfolio

pros: MIT licensed with zero royalties at any revenue level
Scene/node architecture is easier than Unity prefabs
Lightweight editor installs and boots in seconds
cons: C# support lags GDScript on mobile and web targets
3D feature parity with Unreal still a generation behind
Console export relies on third-party paid services
Asset store less mature than Unity Asset Store
review.verdict: Post-Unity-drama, the serious indie default; ship 2D confidently, treat 3D as catching up fast but not there yet.
review.best_for: Indies wanting no royalties, open source, and fast 2D or stylized 3D workflows
review.not_for: Photorealistic AAA 3D or easy first-party console publishing

pricing.high_water_mark_reason: Free/open-source — no commercial pricing, no HWM.
